Root Cause: QA defect lineage can be saved without a test case, breaking requirement→case→run→defect traceability.
Fix Decision: Make source_test_case_id mandatory for QA defect creation from TestHub, or route standalone defects outside QA lineage. (Slice 1)
Test Evidence: manual code read-through; tsc --noEmit 0 errors project-wide
Runtime Evidence: Live-verified via Chrome MCP against localhost:8080 dev server (see catalyst-runtime-proof-report.md).
Notes: Closed in CAT-AUDIT-REMEDIATION-20260705-001 Slice 1.

Root Cause: QA defect lineage can be saved without a test execution run, so failure evidence is optional.
Fix Decision: Require source_test_run_id for defects raised from execution and show the originating run on detail. (Slice 1)
Test Evidence: manual code read-through; tsc --noEmit 0 errors project-wide
Runtime Evidence: Live-verified via Chrome MCP against localhost:8080 dev server (see catalyst-runtime-proof-report.md).
Notes: Closed in CAT-AUDIT-REMEDIATION-20260705-001 Slice 1.

Root Cause: Defect can lack test-plan context, weakening release/test-plan readiness reports.
Fix Decision: Derive or require source_test_plan_id when a defect is created from a cycle/plan.
Notes: Staged for Test Hub remediation wave per docs/audits/catalyst-remediation-plan.md wave order.

Root Cause: The defects list quick-create sends only title/project/default severity, creating standalone QA bugs without case/run/evidence.
Fix Decision: Remove quick-create for QA Bugs or open a guided defect-from-evidence flow. (Slice 1)
Test Evidence: manual code read-through; tsc --noEmit 0 errors project-wide
Runtime Evidence: Live-verified via Chrome MCP against localhost:8080 dev server (see catalyst-runtime-proof-report.md).
Notes: Closed in CAT-AUDIT-REMEDIATION-20260705-001 Slice 1.

Root Cause: Cycle creation adds cases only when selected cases exist, so an empty cycle can be saved.
Fix Decision: Block Create Cycle until at least one approved/published case is selected, or mark it Draft/Not executable. (Slice 1)
Test Evidence: manual code read-through; tsc --noEmit 0 errors project-wide
Runtime Evidence: Live-verified via Chrome MCP against localhost:8080 dev server (see catalyst-runtime-proof-report.md).
Notes: Closed in CAT-AUDIT-REMEDIATION-20260705-001 Slice 1.

Original Finding: No steps defined for this test case — record a case-level verdict instead.
Root Cause: The runner explicitly allows case-level verdict for a test case with no steps; evidence rigor can degrade silently.
Fix Decision: Require steps for formal cases, or make no-step execution an explicit exploratory/session case type.
Notes: Staged for Test Hub remediation wave per docs/audits/catalyst-remediation-plan.md wave order.

Root Cause: Step result rows are only inserted when stepStates exist; no-step cases produce no step-level evidence.
Fix Decision: Show evidence granularity and require actual-result notes/attachments for no-step failed cases.
Notes: Staged for Test Hub remediation wave per docs/audits/catalyst-remediation-plan.md wave order.

Root Cause: SLA breach computation exists but the handover says no user-facing alert/badge/notification is wired.
Fix Decision: Surface live SLA status on dashboard, list, detail, board cards, notifications, and reports.

Original Finding: 6. **Historical MTTR / migration path.** 137 "closed" backfilled incidents have no `resolved_at`/`closed_at` timestamps set (only `status`), so any MTTR calc against the native table for historical incidents will be wrong/empty. Real MTTR d
Root Cause: Closed historical incidents can lack resolved_at/closed_at, making native historical MTTR empty or misleading.
Fix Decision: Add capture-window disclosure and avoid historical MTTR charts until source history exists.

Root Cause: Incident status transition UI risk is documented because frontend lifecycle rules may not match DB constraints.

Original Finding: 5. **Committee Queue page is functional but minimal.** No "Approve"/"Veto" buttons wired in the drawer yet (the mutations exist: `useRecordApproval`, `useRecordVeto`, `useTransitionOnApproval` in `src/hooks/useCommitteeQueue.ts` — page just
Root Cause: The handover documents missing Approve/Veto and Send-to-Committee paths at that point, making governance non-operable without later fixes.
